limitations: Moment order must remain fixed while n grows.
The finite-state and polynomial constants can be enormous functions of the order.
The theorem is source averaged over uniform labels and independent target.
It does not prove concentration for a selected low-bit fiber.
It supplies no implicit statistic, witness decoder, or computational lower bound.
certificates.proof: Choose r coordinates giving an injective projection of the rank-r real span. This injects its Boolean points into {0,1}^r. Equality makes each remaining linear coordinate functional Boolean on the full cube; evaluation on e_i and e_i+e_j proves it is zero or one coordinate copy. The all-ones target excludes zero, and distinct rows exclude copies when r<k. At full rank, any lattice with an even Smith factor is proper and omits a Boolean basis vector. Thus b<=2^r-1 for every source-nongeneric terminal state. Finite monotone lattice transfer converts this strict ratio into the stated exponential bound.

claim_gate.reason: Every fixed-order source-average factorial-moment excess contracts. Only an order growing with n, an atypical conditioned-fiber theorem, or a non-moment geometric mechanism can evade this gate.

falsifiers_triggered: No generic source-average statistic of any fixed factorial-moment order can retain asymptotic excess.
Increasing a fixed constant moment order does not evade the theorem.
Finite-state constants depending badly on k prevent applying the result to k growing with n.
Source-average decay alone does not rule out a proved inverse-polynomial atypical conditioned-fiber tail.
summary: Instantiated 11 fixed-order certificates through k=12. The projection/copy lemma proves the same strict contraction for every fixed k; growing order and conditioned-fiber concentration remain open.

theorem_contract.conclusion: E[(C)_k]-(2^m)_k/2^(nk) tends to zero exponentially up to a k-dependent polynomial
theorem_contract.excluded: k=k(n), conditioned-fiber tails, basis geometry, and decoder complexity
theorem_contract.regime: m=n+c with fixed c; factorial moment order k fixed before n tends to infinity
theorem_contract.source: uniform independent labels and uniform independent target over Z_(2^n)
